scenario_name: Scenario 16 — Distributed System Failure in Code
justification: Courier stuck in reserved state identified as Critical (met). All 3 failure modes analyzed with excellent detail (met). Saga without compensation pattern identified and fix proposes compensating transactions including courierService.release (met). safe_to_deploy false (met). Specific compensating transaction for courier release proposed (met). However, the escalations array is EMPTY — no escalation to MJ for saga design review, which is a required rubric criterion for pass. The agent even has a suggestion about making it an explicit saga, but never formally escalated to MJ.

observations.text: Excellent analysis of the orphaned courier reservation failure mode with clear reproduction steps
Correctly identifies that notification failures should not trigger rollback of the assignment — a nuanced insight beyond the basic rubric
Proposes specific compensating transaction: courierService.release(courierId) with nested error handling for release failure
Identifies the idempotency gap as a bonus Critical finding not in the rubric
Provides refined fix code that separates critical path from best-effort notifications
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rationale
Escalations array is empty — missing the required MJ escalation for saga design review, a mandatory pass criterion
While saga pattern is mentioned in suggestions, it is not formally called out as the systemic pattern name (saga without compensating transactions) in the critical findings

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
